creator: arrowd@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Matthias Andree from comment #12)
> TL;DR: Vetoing to counter maintainer timeout on the removal of my port and replacing it by a port that is "maintained" by a new unknown committer. We must safeguard supply chain security here.

Ouch, sorry, the maintainer would be me. I'll change it in the next update.

> we will need an -exp run and results of all self-test suites ("make test") of affected ports, as well as spot-checking a few ports at runtime.

Agree. I already did a mini exp-run with this too.

> Should we prove a modified patch series good as outlined above, I am offering to adopt the libuuid port.

I also don't mind if you want to be the maintainer. Or maybe we can even co-maintain it, like it was with devel/gammaray: https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-ports/blob/39593510a93f8cadcded94f320496c1c0d798440/devel/gammaray/Makefile#L6-L7
